steps.title: Subtract the two expressions
steps.content:
Use the given values of $x^2$ and $y^2$ directly. The shared $a$ term cancels when the two expressions are subtracted.
$$x^2-y^2=(a+b)-(a+c)$$
$$x^2-y^2=b-c$$

steps.title: Square and expand
steps.content:
Now square that difference and expand the perfect square.
$$(x^2-y^2)^2=(b-c)^2$$
$$(b-c)^2=b^2-2bc+c^2$$
The answer is B.
